title: Array Index Out of Bounds Bug Fix for Missing Max

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find the maximum value. If the array is empty, return -1. The function should return correctly for both arrays with positive and negative numbers, and empty arrays. It is guaranteed that all elements are integers and there are no null values.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes one input: an array of integers. The array size will be between 1 and 10000 (inclusive).
statement.outputFormat: Return -1 if the array is empty; otherwise, return the maximum value in the array.

examples.explanation: Return 5 for an array with one positive number
Return -5 for an array with one negative number
Return -5 for an array with two negative numbers
Return 5 for an array with five numbers

hints.text: Check for the edge case of an empty array first.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: To solve this problem, first check if the array is empty. If it is, return -1 immediately. Otherwise, use a simple loop to find the maximum value.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves using a for loop to find the maximum value.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use the max function to find the maximum value in the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to check if the array is empty and return -1 if it is. Otherwise, use the max function to find the maximum value. The max function will return the maximum value if the array is not empty, avoiding an out of bounds error.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check the edge case of an empty array
Returning null or an incorrect value when the array is not empty
